title: Smoky Chicken Uruguayan Stir-fry
description: A tender and smoky chicken dish prepared with uruguay bouillon cubes, suitable for meal-prep and jain-friendly, featuring a perfect balance of flavors.
instructions: Chop the chicken into bite-sized pieces.
Dice onions and bell peppers into similar sizes as the chicken.
Peel garlic cloves, chop them finely.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add chopped onions and sauté until translucent.
Add the chicken and season with salt and pepper. Cook for about 5 minutes or until golden brown on all sides.
Fold in the bell peppers and continue to cook for another 3-4 minutes, allowing flavors to blend together.
Stir in garlic cubes, let it cook further for a minute or so, then add Uruguay bouillon cubes.
Simmer everything together for about 10 minutes until all liquids have been absorbed. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
